evals.prompt: I want to add Smart Label Capture to my .NET for Android app. Here's my empty Activity: EmptyActivity.cs. My labels are retail price tags — each has a product barcode (EAN-13 and Code 128) and an expiry date, plus an optional total price. I want to read all three fields in one scan and show them.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit NuGet versions, and writes complete Label Capture integration into EmptyActivity.cs: DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey; a manually-managed Camera via Camera.GetDefaultCamera(LabelCapture.RecommendedCameraSettings) + dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync; a label built field-by-field with CustomBarcode.Builder().SetSymbologies(new List<Symbology>{...}).Build(name), ExpiryDateText.Builder()...Build(name), TotalPriceText.Builder().IsOptional(true).Build(name); LabelDefinition.Create(name, fields); LabelCaptureSettings.Create(new List<LabelDefinition>{def}); LabelCapture.Create(context, settings); DataCaptureView.Create(context) added to a container + LabelCaptureBasicOverlay.Create(labelCapture) + dataCaptureView.AddOverlay(overlay); a SessionUpdated event (or ILabelCaptureListener) reading session.CapturedLabels, matching fields by name, reading Barcode?.Data / Text, setting labelCapture.Enabled=false, dispatching via RunOnUiThread; camera on/off + Enabled across the lifecycle. Shows the setup checklist (three NuGet packages incl. Scandit.DataCapture.Label, manifest CAMERA permission, runtime permission, MainApplication with the three Initialize() calls, AppCompat theme, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding Scandit.DataCapture.Core, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode, and Scandit.DataCapture.Label NuGet packages
It does NOT add a separate label-text-models / *.text-models package (text recognizers are bundled in Scandit.DataCapture.Label)
Setup checklist mentions adding android.permission.CAMERA to AndroidManifest.xml
Setup checklist mentions requesting the CAMERA permission at runtime

Setup checklist mentions creating a MainApplication subclass that calls ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize(), ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize(), AND ScanditLabelCapture.Initialize() (all three, required for SDK 8.0+)
Setup checklist mentions using a Theme.AppCompat descendant for the activity
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey( is used to create the context
Each field is built with its own static Builder() factory ending in .Build("name") (e.g. CustomBarcode.Builder()...Build(...), ExpiryDateText.Builder()...Build(...))
The Kotlin-style fluent LabelCaptureSettings.builder()/addLabel()/buildFluent(...) chain is NOT used
CustomBarcode.Builder().SetSymbologies(...) is given an IList<Symbology> (e.g. new List<Symbology>{...}), not a vararg
Symbology.Ean13Upca and Symbology.Code128 are enabled for the barcode field
Kotlin-style underscore symbology names (Symbology.EAN13_UPCA, Symbology.CODE_128) are NOT used
An ExpiryDateText field is created via ExpiryDateText.Builder()
The total price field is created via TotalPriceText.Builder() and marked optional with .IsOptional(true)
LabelDefinition.Create(name, fields) is used to assemble the fields into a definition
LabelCaptureSettings.Create( is called with a list/IList<LabelDefinition> (NOT a settings builder)
LabelCapture.Create(context, settings) is used as the factory (NOT new LabelCapture(...) and NOT LabelCapture.forDataCaptureContext)
A Camera is obtained via Camera.GetDefaultCamera(...) using LabelCapture.RecommendedCameraSettings
dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync( is called with the camera (the camera is managed explicitly)
camera.SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.On) and FrameSourceState.Off drive the camera across the lifecycle
labelCapture.Enabled is set to true so frames are processed
DataCaptureView.Create( is used to create the preview view (a generic DataCaptureView, not a dedicated label view)
LabelCaptureBasicOverlay.Create(labelCapture) is created and added via dataCaptureView.AddOverlay(...)
Either labelCapture.SessionUpdated += handler or labelCapture.AddListener(ILabelCaptureListener) is wired
The handler reads session.CapturedLabels and returns early when it is empty
Fields are matched by their declared Name and values read via Barcode?.Data and Text
labelCapture.Enabled is set to false after a label is captured to avoid re-capturing, and UI work is dispatched via RunOnUiThread
No MAUI APIs or *.Maui packages are introduced (this is a non-MAUI .NET Android project)

evals.prompt: I already have Smart Label Capture working in my .NET Android app (see IntegratedActivity.cs). I need to add one more field to the same label: a lot number printed as plain text that always matches the pattern LOT-[0-9]{6} (e.g. LOT-004821). It's required. Then read it alongside the others.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a CustomText field built with CustomText.Builder().SetValueRegex("LOT-[0-9]{6}").Build("Lot Number"), inserts it into the existing fields list, and reads it via field Text in OnSessionUpdated, preserving the rest of the integration. It uses SetValueRegex (NOT the old native setPattern/setDataTypePattern).

assertions.text: A CustomText field is created via CustomText.Builder()...Build("<name>")
The value pattern is set with SetValueRegex("LOT-[0-9]{6}") (or SetValueRegexes with that pattern)
The old native names setPattern / setDataTypePattern / setPatterns are NOT used
The new field is added to the existing List<LabelFieldDefinition> (it is NOT marked optional, since it is required)
The lot number value is read from the captured label via field.Text matching its declared name
Existing integration code (camera, LabelCapture.Create, DataCaptureView, overlay, SessionUpdated handler, existing barcode and expiry fields) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to add Smart Label Capture to my app. Here's my MauiProgram.cs. I need to scan a barcode and an expiry date.
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes that MauiProgram.cs indicates a .NET MAUI project (UseMauiApp / MauiApp.CreateBuilder), which is out of scope for this non-MAUI .NET Android skill. It stops and tells the user this skill does not apply to MAUI and points them to the MAUI integration path, rather than writing non-MAUI Activity-based integration code into a MAUI app.

assertions.text: The response identifies the project as a .NET MAUI app (e.g. references MauiApp.CreateBuilder / UseMauiApp / MauiProgram)
The response explicitly states this skill / the non-MAUI .NET Android integration does not apply to MAUI
It does NOT write Activity-based non-MAUI integration code (no [Activity], DataCaptureView.Create + AddView, AppCompatActivity) into the MAUI project
It points the user toward the MAUI integration path (e.g. a MAUI-specific skill / hosting the view as a XAML element)

evals.prompt: I already have Smart Label Capture working in my .NET Android app (see IntegratedActivity.cs) using the basic overlay. I want to change how detected fields are highlighted in the camera feed: draw the expiry date field box in a different color than the barcode field box, and hide the box around the whole label. Keep the rest of my integration as-is.
evals.expected_output: The skill customizes the LabelCaptureBasicOverlay. It either sets the overlay brush properties globally (CapturedFieldBrush / PredictedFieldBrush / LabelBrush) and/or implements an ILabelCaptureBasicOverlayListener returning a Brush? per field (BrushForField matching by the field Name) and per label (BrushForLabel), assigns it via overlay.Listener, and uses new Brush(fillColor, strokeColor, strokeWidth) from Scandit.DataCapture.Core.UI.Style. It hides the whole-label box with Brush.TransparentBrush (or by returning a transparent brush from BrushForLabel). The existing camera/context/label definition/SessionUpdated wiring is preserved.

assertions.text: The basic overlay highlight is customized either via overlay brush properties (CapturedFieldBrush / PredictedFieldBrush / LabelBrush) or via an ILabelCaptureBasicOverlayListener

If a listener is used, it implements ILabelCaptureBasicOverlayListener with BrushForField(LabelCaptureBasicOverlay, LabelField, CapturedLabel) and BrushForLabel(LabelCaptureBasicOverlay, CapturedLabel) and is assigned via overlay.Listener
The expiry field is distinguished from the barcode field by matching field.Name (the same names passed to .Build("..."))
A Brush is constructed with new Brush(fillColor, strokeColor, strokeWidth) (the three-argument Brush constructor)
The whole-label box is hidden using Brush.TransparentBrush (NOT a method call Brush.Transparent())
A per-field/per-label override returns null to fall back to the default brush rather than throwing or returning a hardcoded default
The existing camera, LabelCapture.Create, DataCaptureView, and SessionUpdated wiring is preserved

evals.prompt: I already have Smart Label Capture working in my .NET Android app (see IntegratedActivity.cs). On top of the normal highlighting, I want to draw my own Android view — a small 'Expires soon!' badge — anchored just below the expiry date field whenever it's detected. Keep my basic overlay for the highlight boxes.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a LabelCaptureAdvancedOverlay alongside the existing LabelCaptureBasicOverlay. It creates it with LabelCaptureAdvancedOverlay.Create(labelCapture), adds it via dataCaptureView.AddOverlay(advancedOverlay), and assigns an ILabelCaptureAdvancedOverlayListener. The listener returns a custom Android View (e.g. a TextView) from ViewForCapturedLabelField for the expiry field (matched by Name/Type) and null otherwise, supplies an Anchor (e.g. Anchor.BottomCenter) and a PointWithUnit offset (with MeasureUnit) for the field, and returns null from ViewForCapturedLabel so no whole-label view is drawn. The existing basic overlay and integration are preserved.

assertions.text: A LabelCaptureAdvancedOverlay is created via LabelCaptureAdvancedOverlay.Create(labelCapture)
The advanced overlay is added to the DataCaptureView via dataCaptureView.AddOverlay(...)
A class implementing ILabelCaptureAdvancedOverlayListener is assigned via advancedOverlay.Listener
ViewForCapturedLabelField returns a custom Android View (e.g. a TextView) for the expiry field, matched by field.Name (and optionally field.Type == LabelFieldType.Text), and null for other fields
AnchorForCapturedLabelField returns an Anchor (e.g. Anchor.BottomCenter) and OffsetForCapturedLabelField returns a PointWithUnit using a MeasureUnit (e.g. MeasureUnit.Dip)
ViewForCapturedLabel returns null so no whole-label AR view is drawn
The existing LabelCaptureBasicOverlay is kept (both overlays attach to the same DataCaptureView)

evals.prompt: I want to add Smart Label Capture to my .NET for Android app. Here's my empty Activity: EmptyActivity.cs. The labels are hard-disk-drive asset stickers: each has a serial number barcode, a part number barcode, and the device IMEI printed as a barcode. Read all three.
evals.expected_output: The skill builds the label definition out of the pre-built semantic barcode fields rather than raw CustomBarcode: SerialNumberBarcode.Builder().Build(name), PartNumberBarcode.Builder().Build(name), and ImeiOneBarcode.Builder().Build(name) (ImeiTwoBarcode for the second IMEI segment if relevant). These come with preset symbologies/regexes, so it does not re-specify them unless the user asks. It assembles them with LabelDefinition.Create(name, fields) and LabelCaptureSettings.Create(...), and reads each value via field.Barcode?.Data matching the declared name. The rest of the integration (context, camera, view, basic overlay, SessionUpdated) follows the standard pattern.

assertions.text: A serial-number field is built with SerialNumberBarcode.Builder()...Build("<name>")
A part-number field is built with PartNumberBarcode.Builder()...Build("<name>")
An IMEI field is built with ImeiOneBarcode.Builder()...Build("<name>") (ImeiTwoBarcode if a second IMEI segment is needed)
The pre-built semantic fields are used instead of generic CustomBarcode for serial/part/IMEI (their preset symbologies and regexes are relied on, not re-specified)
The fields are assembled via LabelDefinition.Create(name, fields) and LabelCaptureSettings.Create(new List<LabelDefinition>{...})
Each value is read from the captured label via field.Barcode?.Data matching its declared Name
Kotlin-style factory or fluent forms (e.g. SerialNumberBarcode.fieldWithName / addSerialNumberBarcode().buildFluent()) are NOT used

evals.prompt: I'm building a retail .NET Android app and I want Smart Label Capture to read standard price tags without me hand-defining every field. Here's my empty Activity: EmptyActivity.cs. Use whatever pre-built support exists for price labels.
evals.expected_output: The skill uses the pre-built price-capture label definition instead of manually building fields: LabelDefinition.CreatePriceCaptureDefinition("<name>"), then LabelCaptureSettings.Create(new List<LabelDefinition>{ priceLabel }). It wires up the rest of the integration (context, camera, DataCaptureView, LabelCaptureBasicOverlay, SessionUpdated reading fields by name via Text / Barcode?.Data) as usual. It does NOT pair the pre-built price definition with the Validation Flow overlay (documented as incompatible).

assertions.text: The pre-built price definition is created via LabelDefinition.CreatePriceCaptureDefinition("<name>")
It is passed to LabelCaptureSettings.Create(new List<LabelDefinition>{ ... }) (NOT a settings builder)
Manual field-by-field building (CustomBarcode/TotalPriceText/UnitPriceText) is NOT used when the pre-built definition covers the need
The pre-built price definition is NOT combined with LabelCaptureValidationFlowOverlay (it is documented as VF-incompatible); the basic overlay is used
Kotlin-style createPriceCaptureLabel / forPriceCapture naming is NOT used (the .NET factory is CreatePriceCaptureDefinition)

evals.prompt: I want Smart Label Capture in my .NET Android app to read a seven-segment display (a digital meter readout). Here's my empty Activity: EmptyActivity.cs. Is there built-in support, and how do I wire it up?
evals.expected_output: The skill uses the pre-built seven-segment-display label definition: LabelDefinition.CreateSevenSegmentDisplayLabelDefinition("<name>"), passed to LabelCaptureSettings.Create(new List<LabelDefinition>{ ... }). It wires the rest of the integration (context, camera, DataCaptureView, basic overlay, SessionUpdated reading the field value via Text) as usual. It does not hand-build a CustomText field with a digits regex when the pre-built definition exists.

assertions.text: The pre-built seven-segment definition is created via LabelDefinition.CreateSevenSegmentDisplayLabelDefinition("<name>")
It is passed to LabelCaptureSettings.Create(new List<LabelDefinition>{ ... })
The captured value is read from the label field via field.Text (matching the declared Name)
The standard integration scaffolding (DataCaptureContext, Camera.GetDefaultCamera, DataCaptureView.Create, LabelCaptureBasicOverlay.Create, SessionUpdated/AddListener) is present

evals.prompt: My customer scans worn, faded shipping labels and the on-device OCR sometimes misses the expiry date, forcing warehouse staff to type it. I'm already using the Validation Flow in my .NET Android app. Is there a cloud option that improves recognition, and how do I turn it on?
evals.expected_output: The skill explains the Adaptive Recognition Engine (ARE) — a cloud-based OCR fallback that escalates frames the on-device model can't read to a larger cloud model. It flags that ARE is in Beta, requires a license key with the ARE feature flag (contact support@scandit.com for production; trial keys for evaluation), and works ONLY through the Validation Flow. It shows the enabling API: setting LabelDefinition.AdaptiveRecognitionMode = AdaptiveRecognitionMode.Auto on the existing label definition (a single added line), keeping the rest of the VF setup unchanged.

assertions.text: The response defines ARE as the Adaptive Recognition Engine, a cloud-based OCR fallback that triggers when the on-device model fails
The response states ARE is in Beta
The response states ARE requires a license key with the ARE feature flag and that production use requires contacting support@scandit.com
The response states ARE works only with the Validation Flow (not the basic or advanced overlay)
The enabling API is shown as LabelDefinition.AdaptiveRecognitionMode = AdaptiveRecognitionMode.Auto on the label definition
Kotlin/iOS-style forms (.adaptiveRecognition(.auto), setAdaptiveRecognitionMode underscore enums) are NOT used
